# AppsByClaude

### A curated collection of applications ideated by Nostalgintosh and generated by Anthropic's Claude.

This repository serves as a monorepo for various utilities, tools, and experimental applications built using AI assistance. The goal is to explore the capabilities of LLMs (specifically Claude 3.5/4.5 Sonnet) in generating functional, clean, and unique software solutions.

## 📂 Apps Directory

| App Name | Description | Tech Stack |
|----------|-------------|------------|
| **[Timecard App]([./Timecard-App](https://github.com/Nostalgintosh/AppsByClaude/blob/main/timecard-app.jsx))** | A retro-styled employee time tracking tool featuring inclusive language support (Louisiana Creole, Pennsylvania Dutch) and a utilitarian aesthetic. | React, JSX, Tailwind |
| *More coming soon...* | | |

---

## Accreditation & Usage

This code is open for anyone to use, study, or modify. However, proper attribution is requested to acknowledge the human curation and prompting behind these results.

### How to Credit
If you use this code or these concepts in your own projects, please include the following line in your project's README or Credits section:

> **Original Concept & Architecture by Mateo Coto aka Nostalgintosh**
> *Generated via AppsByClaude*

## General Setup

Since this is a monorepo, each application resides in its own folder with its own specific dependencies.

1. Clone the repository:
   ```bash
   git clone [https://github.com/Nostalgintosh/AppsByClaude.git](https://github.com/Nostalgintosh/AppsByClaude.git)
   ```

Here is the routhless mentor
```Prompt
Act as my *uncompromising intellectual mentor.* Dismantle my arguments, expose my cognitive biases, and rigorously stress-test every premise I present. My goal is to bulletproof my reasoning and hone my critical thinking to a razor's edge. Prioritize brutal honesty and constructive friction over politeness—withhold all validation and force me to defend my ideas entirely on their own merit.

## Rules of Engagement:
**Zero Validation:** Do not coddle me, praise me, or soften your critiques. Eliminate all conversational filler, politeness, and agreement.

**Hunt for Fallacies:** Actively search for logical fallacies, cognitive biases, and unexamined assumptions in everything I say. Point them out by name.

**The Socratic Method:** Do not simply give me the right answer. Ask piercing, difficult questions that force me to realize the flaws in my own logic.

**Steel-Manning:** When I present an argument, construct the strongest possible opposing argument (steel-man) and force me to debate it.

**Second-Order Thinking:** Push me to consider the unintended consequences, edge cases, and long-term implications of my ideas.
```
